quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: Interior and Exterior Angle Sum Formulas
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 6.1 Properties of Polygons and Angles

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a hexagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of an $n$-gon is $(n - 2) \times 180^\circ$. For a hexagon, $n = 6$: $(6 - 2) \times 180^\circ = 4 \times 180^\circ = 720^\circ$.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a decagon?
The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is $1260^\circ$. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Using $(n - 2) \times 180^\circ = 1260^\circ$: $n - 2 = 7$, so $n = 9$. The polygon is a nonagon.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular octagon?
questions.explanation: For a regular octagon ($n = 8$), the interior angle sum is $(8 - 2) \times 180^\circ = 1080^\circ$. Each interior angle measures $1080^\circ \div 8 = 135^\circ$.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: For a regular pentagon ($n = 5$), the interior angle sum is $(5 - 2) \times 180^\circ = 540^\circ$. Each interior angle measures $540^\circ \div 5 = 108^\circ$.

questions.question: What is the sum of the exterior angles of any convex polygon, one at each vertex?
questions.explanation: The sum of the exterior angles of any convex polygon, taking one exterior angle at each vertex, is always $360^\circ$, regardless of the number of sides.

questions.question: What is the measure of each exterior angle of a regular nonagon?
questions.explanation: For any regular polygon, each exterior angle measures $360^\circ \div n$. For a nonagon ($n = 9$): $360^\circ \div 9 = 40^\circ$.

questions.question: Each exterior angle of a regular polygon measures $24^\circ$. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle of a regular polygon measures $360^\circ \div n$. So $360^\circ \div n = 24^\circ$, giving $n = 360^\circ \div 24^\circ = 15$.

questions.question: Each interior angle of a regular polygon measures $156^\circ$. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle is $156^\circ$, so each exterior angle is $180^\circ - 156^\circ = 24^\circ$. The number of sides is $360^\circ \div 24^\circ = 15$.

questions.question: A polygon has $n$ sides. Which expression represents the sum of its interior angles?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of an $n$-gon is $(n - 2) \times 180^\circ$. This formula comes from dividing the polygon into $n - 2$ triangles, each contributing $180^\circ$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the interior and exterior angle sum formulas for polygons and can apply them to find unknown side counts and angle measures.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the formulas but review converting between interior and exterior angle measures for regular polygons.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: interior angle sum = $(n-2) \times 180^\circ$, exterior angle sum = $360^\circ$, and each exterior angle of a regular polygon = $360^\circ \div n$.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The interior angle sum formula is $(n-2) \times 180^\circ$. The exterior angle sum is always $360^\circ$. For regular polygons, each interior angle = $(n-2) \times 180^\circ \div n$.
